2606.08360.title: Generative Frontier Planning for Adaptive Peer-Referral Recruitment under Covariate-Dependent Arrivals
2606.08360.paper_summary: Peer-referral recruitment systems such as respondent-driven sampling are critical for studying and intervening on hidden populations affected by infectious diseases. To accelerate recruitment, public health agencies must adaptively allocate limited referral resources across multiple rounds, where current decisions shape both the number and the covariates of future recruits. Prior work makes this problem tractable by assuming that referrals are drawn i.i.d.\ from a homogeneous population, an assumption that ignores the homophily and shared context that drive real peer recruitment. We instead consider a more realistic model in which both referral capacity and the covariates of newly referred individuals are conditioned on the referrer, learned from data with a censored count model and a conditional generative model. The resulting planning problem is challenging because each candidate allocation induces a different distribution over future recruits. We propose \emph{Generative Frontier Planning} (GFP), a model-based planner that replaces per-step Monte-Carlo sampling with a deterministic backup over a latent covariate-coverage value surrogate. The surrogate is designed so that the expected value of the next frontier depends on the offspring generative model only through finite-dimensional summaries that are amortized offline, and so that the resulting per-round objective is monotone with diminishing returns. Together, these two properties make planning tractable: the deterministic backup eliminates Monte-Carlo sampling, and the diminishing-returns structure lets a marginal greedy allocation achieve a \((1-1/e)\)-approximation for the per-round problem. On a simulation environment calibrated to a real respondent-driven sampling dataset, GFP outperforms random, reinforcement-learning, and i.i.d.\ dynamic-programming baselines across four discount factors.
2606.08360.paper_summary_zh: 同儕推薦招募系統，例如受訪者驅動抽樣，對於研究和干預受傳染病影響的隱藏族群至關重要。為了加速招募，公共衛生機構必須在多輪中自適應地分配有限的推薦資源，當前的決策會影響未來招募人數及其協變量。先前的研究通過假設推薦是從同質族群中獨立同分佈（i.i.d.）抽取的，使這個問題變得可處理，這一假設忽略了驅動真實同儕招募的同質性和共享背景。我們則考慮一個更現實的模型，其中推薦能力和新推薦個體的協變量都依賴於推薦者，這是通過帶有截斷計數模型和條件生成模型的數據學習得出的。由此產生的規劃問題具有挑戰性，因為每個候選分配都會對未來的招募產生不同的分佈。我們提出了\emph{生成邊界規劃}（GFP），這是一種基於模型的規劃器，將每步的蒙特卡羅抽樣替換為對潛在協變量覆蓋值代理的確定性備份。該代理的設計使得下一個邊界的期望值僅通過有限維的摘要依賴於後代生成模型，並且使得最終每輪的目標是單調的，並具有遞減收益。這兩個特性使得規劃變得可處理：確定性備份消除了蒙特卡羅抽樣，而遞減收益結構使得邊際貪婪分配能夠實現每輪問題的\((1-1/e)\)-近似。在一個與真實受訪者驅動抽樣數據集校準的模擬環境中，GFP在四個折扣因子下超越了隨機、增強學習和i.i.d.動態規劃基準。
2606.08360.author: Lingkai Kong et.al.
2606.08360.authors: Lingkai Kong, Hezi Jiang, Andrew Ma, Keyu Wang, Akseli Kangaslahti, Milind Tambe
